常見問題：

AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。

AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。

今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。

簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥)

"I see the lads want to do more, they want to prove they belong at Milan and that is creating a psychological block. It is my job to unblock it.

"It is a growth process and I am convinced we will get there because most of these players have never worn a jersey like this before and the situation is making them struggle to express themselves."

AC最近喺意甲聯賽已經連輸3場，到周四歐霸盃分組賽，主場迎戰AEK雅典再贏唔到，0:0悶和收場，教練「飛機仔」蒙迪拿賽後就對班球迷有微言，認為唔應該見低就踩，今場波不斷噓球隊。

AC主場聖西路球場可以容納八萬人，但呢場波只得大約17,000人入場，見班球員都係冇乜起色，遲遲入唔到波，實行向支持球隊柴台。蒙迪拿賽後話：「我哋目前處於困難階段，球迷喺場上嘅反應，相信會令球員更加難受，佢哋咁樣做，唔係因為呢場波嘅表現好唔好，而係發洩之前喺意甲連輸3場嘅不滿。」

feel到今場係懵撚既沒日

今晚對熱拿亞的新聞發佈會，蒙迪拿說：我想看到球隊比賽中的勇氣，我們需要飢餓和欲望，反彈很重要。

熱拿亞不好對付，他們會讓你踢得很差因為他們採用高位壓迫，盯人防守。他們可以贏得或輸給任何球隊，所以我們必須精明，勇敢。這不會是一場控球為主的比賽，因為熱拿亞不會讓我們這樣做，所以我想像這將是一場有些混亂的比賽，我們必須冷靜。

我們每三天進行一場比賽，所以我必須做出改變，你不可能總是派出同樣的球員。

是的，我犯了一些錯誤。對於我的職業來說這很正常，但我很樂觀，我們繼續努力尋找解決方案。球隊變化這麼大，哥迪奧拿和摩連奴在他們的第一年不也是沒成功嗎。

是的，我們在積分榜上落後，但我重復一遍，數據顯示只有拿玻里有比我們有更多的射門。我理解球迷們的失望，但這是因為比賽的結果，而不是因為我們的表現。如果那天我們擊敗了國米，我就會被視為一個天才。

我還是和第一天一樣，很高興成為米蘭的教練，但我也知道這個行業固有的風險。我的一生都是一個冒險的風險。我很年輕的時候就離開家園，夢想成為一名球員，我開始在羅馬當教練時非常年輕，幾年以后接手了費倫天拿。風險並不會嚇倒我，這是我盡全力的地方。

卡連歷情況不錯他明天會出戰，而安達施華今年入了很多球，作為一名前鋒人們對他期望很多，但他只有21歲。

眼下我不關心聯賽的積分榜。我專注於下一場比賽，我們會一步一步來，我和米拿貝利沒有任何問題。

我接受對我們的批評，但我不能全部認同。

我很清楚，有動力。我想引用丘吉爾的名言：, «success is the ability to go from failure to failure without losing your enthusiasm»."成功是從失敗到另一個失敗的能力，而不失去熱情。"

AC Milan have decided the future of Vincenzo Montella, according to reports.
The Italian tactician has had a disappointing start to the season and the rossoneri are without a win in the last five games in all competitions.

AC Milan are well out of Champions League placements, the club’s minimum target.
According to Sky Sport, however, the rossoneri are not going to sack Montella after the rossoneri draw against Genoa.

AC Milan are due to play many games in the coming weeks and the board of AC Milan do not want to change the manager in the middle of such a busy moment.

The decision of the rossoneri side comes after weeks of speculations over the future of the Aeroplanino.

The likes of Walter Mazzarri and Gennaro Gattuso are being linked with replacing Montella at the San Siro but if the report of Sky Sport is accurate, AC Milan won’t sack their manager after the home Genoa draw.

4. SUSO 8號戰術, 起右邊 1on 1/ 1on 2落底傳/ 洛賓射法似我/ 左腳斬入去/ 傅12碼

6. 打少個都唔失波, 又可以做藉口, 如果有11個人蒙帥已經多番2分
7. 運氣(上季有幾場應該輸既波已經用晒QUOTA)

Bonucci offers to return armband?

Leonardo Bonucci reportedly offered to return Milan’s captaincy to Riccardo Montolivo after the Rossoneri’s 2-0 defeat to Sampdoria last month.

According to Gazzetta dello Sport and La Repubblica, Bonucci told his teammates, “if I’m the problem, the armband is here,” in the dressing room at Marassi.

Bonucci would then have tried to return the armband to Montolivo, who had it before the defender, but “received a negative response.”

The former Juventus stopper will miss the Milan’s next two games after his red card against Genoa on Sunday.

A imprensa italiana escreve esta terça-feira que Jorge Jesus poderá suceder a Vincenzo Montella no cargo de treinador do AC Milan.

O site desportivo italiano Tuttomercato escreve mesmo que o diretor desportivo do Milan terá mesmo viajado para Lisboa para se encontrar com o representante do técnico do Sporting.

Alessandro Costacurta believes Milan’s problem is the lack of a striker “like Andrea Belotti or Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang.”

The Rossoneri started strong, but have now managed one point from the last four Serie A rounds.

“With someone like Belotti or Aubameyang upfront, we’d be talking about a very different Milan,” the former Rossoneri defender said on Sky Sport Italia, where he is a regular pundit.

“It is objectively clear that what this Diavolo lacks is a quality centre-forward, because they do get the crosses in and their strikers make a lot of mistakes in front of goal.”

Vincenzo Montella’s men have failed to find the net in four of their last six competitive matches.

They had been heavily linked with both the Torino striker and Borussia Dortmund star, but the price-tag cited was close to €100m.

Montella: '€100m players don't come'



Vincenzo Montella hinted Milan wanted to sign a top striker this summer, but “€100m players don’t come without the Champions League.”

The Rossoneri spent over €200m in the transfer window, including €38m for Andre Silva and €25m for Nikola Kalinic, yet wasted several great chances in today’s 2-0 defeat to Juventus.

“Not everyone has a Gonzalo Higuain,” said Montella in his Press conference. “The match was decided by a top player who created two goals out of nothing.

“Top players worth €100m won’t come without the Champions League. I am happy with the transfer market we conducted.”

Milan had been linked with the likes of Torino’s Andrea Belotti and Borussia Dortmund hitman Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, but eventually signed Kalinic from Fiorentina.

“I don’t envy anything that Juventus have. They set the example as a team built over time. They are experienced and are now introducing some young players, but they are built to win the Champions League.

“There was optimism over the summer because big achievements are possible if you approach them the right way, even if some incidents could’ve gone our way.”

Montella was also asked about the disappointing performance of former Lazio midfielder Lucas Biglia, who repeatedly gave the ball away.

“I don’t want to comment on individuals. I do think he can do better. He’s an extraordinary player, but I don’t know why, he has recently not looked calm. I am convinced he’ll become the added value we are looking for.”

Maldini: 'Milan not top four material yet'



Paolo Maldini urges Milan to have patience, especially against Juventus, but “they are not worthy of the top four right now.”

“It’s going to be a tough one because Juve are the strongest squad in Italy,” the Rossoneri legend told Sky Sport Italia.

“Milan have revamped the squad and it’s going to require a little patience. After so many changes, results never come immediately.

“They are not worthy of the top four now, but it’s a long season ahead. So far other teams have played better and deserve to be above them.”
